en.extract:
The white-lined honeyeater (Meliphaga albilineata) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to northern Australia.  Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It formerly included the Kimberley honeyeater as a subspecies.

The '''white-lined honeyeater''' ('''''Meliphaga albilineata''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to northern [[Australia]].  Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It formerly included the [[Kimberley honeyeater]] as a [[subspecies]].
